textDisplay:
@fantastic9449 I don’t care about whatever this guy means by “woke”, but you have to admit that a lot of movies and shows coming out right now that are supposedly about a lead male character, and it’s usually an already established character, are making it a point to have a female counterpart to the main male lead. Which is fine every once in a while, but it’s happening so much it can’t not be noticed. To name some very recent mainstream ones, the new James Bond, the new Thor, the Loki series, the Kenobi series, the new Doctor Strange (slightly different, but it counts in my opinion) and this is me just half asleep naming a few recent ones off the top of my head. There are many more. 

It sucks, because there is unfortunately a very fine line between being naturally diverse, and then pushing diversity in a blatant way. I want diversity in stories, I don’t want stories that are written with “being diverse” in mind.

textDisplay: One of Pixar's finest trailers as of late. I love that this one seems geared for an older audience, who's nostalgic for the Toy Story Buzz Lightyear. The trailer feels so emotional and exciting. It really sets a beautiful and intriguing tone for the movie without revealing much. I really dislike Pixar's recent trailers because they seem to be overly childish and very misleading. After seeing Coco's trailer, I thought the movie would be very goofy and subpar. But that movie was the COMPLETE opposite - filled with heart and meaning in every scene. Same with Turning Red. Didn't love the trailer, but I just trusted Pixar on that one. Hopefully this trailer raises the bar instead of raising expectations that it can't meet haha. I doubt it'll be the latter but you never know!
textOriginal: One of Pixar's finest trailers as of late. I love that this one seems geared for an older audience, who's nostalgic for the Toy Story Buzz Lightyear. But that movie was the COMPLETE opposite - filled with heart and meaning in every scene. Same with Turning Red. Didn't love the trailer, but I just trusted Pixar on that one. Hopefully this trailer raises the bar instead of raising expectations that it can't meet haha. I doubt it'll be the latter but you never know!

textDisplay: @Panimal98 i'm mostly focused on the eras these movies belong to and the feelings they invoke more than anything. and the impressions gathered from seeing the movie itself, because I don't know about you but I do not remember andy ever talking about a "Lightyear" movie and that being what inspired his obsession with buzz. the impression i had from watching toy story (and i'm sure you did too before these trailers popped up) was that buzz was simply the hot new toy on the market, who had these impressive and innovative new features (by a kid in 1995's standards) like a light-up wrist and deployable wings that made woody look antiquated by comparison.

textDisplay:
Who is Buzz Lightyear?

We know of The Space Ranger, the Team Leader, the Toy from Andy’s childhood, the one foretold by Martian myths and legends.

But then who *is* Buzz Lightyear?

Is he the kind of man who dreamed of going to space, to be the first of many to set out and meet other life forms?  Is he a soldier who does what he’s told, but acts out if his heart says otherwise?  Or is he just a man like any other?

These are the questions I have after seeing this.  And I can’t wait to see how Pixar breaks us at an emotional level 😂
textOriginal:
